claim: The text-only section (entropy 3.9016 bits, highest of all sections) contains unenciphered or minimally enciphered natural language prose, distinguishable from other sections by significantly higher conditional entropy H(glyph | preceding glyph) that approaches values for unenciphered medieval Latin or Italian.
evidence_for: Entropy 3.9016 bits is closest to Latin (~4.0) and Italian (~3.95) of all sections per current beliefs
Prior belief [0.622]/[0.673] already supports this at moderate confidence
Text-only section has 7 folios with no illustrations, suggesting different compositional intent â€” possibly a key, index, or plaintext introduction
evidence_against: Small sample (2,349 words / 7 folios) limits statistical power
Entropy difference from other sections is only ~0.14 bits, within range of cipher-induced variation
test: Compute second-order conditional entropy H2 = H(g_i | g_{i-1}) for text-only section and compare to all other sections. Additionally compute mutual information MI(g_i; g_{i+2}) to detect skip-bigram structure. Benchmark against MI values computed from enciphered Latin (simple substitution applied to a medieval Latin corpus) and plaintext Latin.
test_metric: Second-order conditional glyph entropy H2 and skip-bigram mutual information MI(g_i; g_{i+2}) for text-only vs. all other sections vs. enciphered/plaintext Latin benchmarks
test_threshold: If text-only H2 is > 1.5 bits and within 0.1 bits of plaintext Latin H2, and significantly higher than other Voynich sections, minimal-encipherment hypothesis is supported

reasoning: If confirmed, the text-only section becomes the primary decipherment target; the differential H2 signature between sections would also reveal which sections share the same encoding method, partitioning the problem.
